BONUS: Nightmare Story
Live Q&A
ALL CONTENT © COPYRIGHT 2011 AND BEYOND | JON BENSON 3X

Josh: 	How do they get a nightmare story?
Jon: 	Most people have one. If you don’t have one, then obviously a nightmare story
doesn’t work. So, you have to turn it into a nightmare story for your clients.
I’ve seen a video sales letter that did this very well. They told a nightmare story
about a woman named Jan. This is how they related to Jan.
Basically, it was similar to my story. It was a weight loss product and they went
into one of their stories about Jan.
This was because the guy that made this product is one of those guys that we
all hate, that has ripped abs no matter what they eat. Personally, I think you
should be fat and then write a diet book, once you’re lean. I don’t know. But
I’m getting off on another soapbox.
So, if you don’t have a nightmare story that you can really discover really
quickly, I bet one of your clients do. Of course, change the name. Or one of
your friends that have been through something similar.
But if anyone throws out an industry to me right now that they think, “I don’t
think this will work for me,” I’ll show you how it can. So, that’s the challenge.
Josh: 	Someone says that their audience is typically in a huge hurry, and they want a
solution really quickly. How do you deal with that, when your VSL is a long
VSL?
Jon: 	We’ve tested every industry you can think of, and we’ve tested different links of
VSL’s. Rarely – and I do mean rarely – does a short VSL out-perform a long
one, no matter what your industry is. It just doesn’t.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 11 --

2
Now, you may have a unique industry. So, what we’re going to do when we
start putting the VSL together in PowerPoint, is you’re going to create multiple
versions. One will be shorter, one will be longer. And you’ll test. We’re going
to get into how to test in later sessions. We’re not even close to being there
yet.
But if you think that that’s a concern, then start off with a 15-minute. That’s the
bare-bones minimum that I suggest, a 15-minute VSL, and then you can take
out some of the story elements, if you think that that is necessary. Or, you can
just write a shorter story.
Personally, I think that no matter what you sell, you need to build rapport. No
matter what. No matter what.
Even someone that’s famous, I would say this about. Like at Tony Robbins’
website, he still builds rapport. Most people know who Tony Robbins is. But
he still goes into a whole rapport story.
So, no matter what it is, I suggest you build rapport. You can build it shorter.
But we found the most successful sales letters are around that 30-minute mark.
But we’ll talk about how to shorten it when we start putting them together.
Josh: 	Should we develop a different story for each product or service itself?
Jon: 	I would suggest it. The exercise here, when you practice something, you get
better at it. Right? So, if you have 3 or 4 products that are totally differently –
obviously, they’d have to be different – it would be great practice to write 3 or 4
nightmare stories. As you write them, you’ll just get better and better. And
you’ll go, “Wow, that’s really good! I’m going to throw that in the other one.”
So, yeah, that’s a great exercise. Creative writing is something that people do
naturally. I’m not concerned with those people. I’m concerned with the people
that can’t do it. I know there’s copywriters on this course, and that’s great. I
love you guys. But you guys can write copy and it kind of flows naturally. But I
guarantee you, every copywriter listening to me right now will tell you that the
more they practice, the better they get. Go figure.
If someone says, “Wow, what a natural,” chances are they practiced.
So, this is a great way to practice: write 2 or 3 nightmare stories. Like I said, I
have 2 that I use. There were 2 events in my life that caused me to change.
Josh: 	Would there be times that a nightmare story doesn’t work for whatever your
product or service is?
Jon: 	Cindy, can you give me a specific example?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 11 --

3
Josh: 	How do you present the nightmare story when you’re doing it from your client’s
point of view? How do you get into that with a leeway, leverage point?
Jon: 	That’s actually pretty easy. Let’s go back and look on the screen here. Let’s
go back and look at how I started this. I’m just going to change this exact story
to somebody else, on the fly. Let’s make this Steve. I’ll make Steve the culprit
here.
“I remember when my friend Steve decided that enough was enough. I’ve got
permission to share his story with you now, even though he admits it’s really
embarrassing. But I think you need to know how deep down the rabbit hole
some people can go. I kind of went there myself, before discovering the stuff
that I’m going to share with you today, that allows me and Steve to lead this
really amazing life.”
You see how easy that is? All I did was put somebody else into the nightmare
story position.
Now, of course, if you’re telling the story about a real person, you always want
to run this by them. Hopefully, it is a real person, and you should change their
name, of course.
Like I said, one of the weight loss products that I know wrote a story about a
woman named Jan. It was a very moving story. A very, very moving story.
So, it does not have to be about you. I just think it’s better if it is. It’s not a
make-or-break on conversions, but it gets much more of a building of rapport if
you’re telling a story about yourself. Because, again, your goal here is to
become the reluctant expert. That’s your goal. Maybe that’s a better focal
point for the question, Josh.
Is there anyone out there that doesn’t think that they can become the reluctant
expert, the expert by necessity, that you were just naturally a bad-ass at
whatever you do and you’ve never had a down moment in your life? If anyone
believes that, let me know. It may be true, but let’s find out.
Everybody’s gone through some sort of dark time in their life, where they
weren’t as good as they are right now. And that’s all you need to tell.
Now, weight loss is an extremely emotional subject. Something that isn’t an
emotional subject is a friend of mine owns a sex toy company. Not too
emotional. Not a lot of room for nightmare stories. Or is there? Because there
is. There is. And we’ve talked about this. Hit the site, you buy the toy. Right?
He made this guy up. This guy was completely fictional. In this case, it doesn’t
matter. He said, “Joey was like the worst lover ever. He could not hold onto a
girlfriend to save his life. And then…” It’s obvious what he discovered. “And
then, he had this one girlfriend that turned him on to blah, blah, blah.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 11 --

4
So, that’s his nightmare story. It doesn’t have anything to do with him, it has to
do with this fictional character named Joey that he created out of thin, blue sky.
Like I said, I personally prefer being honest and having it being about yourself.
But that’s my preference.
Josh: 	We’ve got lots of people who are under the impression that the nightmare story
wouldn’t work for their product, just like you just explained for the sex toy
example.
Okay, “I have a product where I want to help people learn how to market
themselves, to get attention in a very difficult market.” Oh, man, that one’s
wide open. That one’s really, really wide open.
Jon: 	You can tell a story of yourself. You can say, “My business, I was lost. I didn’t
have any idea how to get attention in this extremely difficult market. In fact, I
was getting lost like a dime on a highway.”
That’s really almost all you have to say. You don’t have to build some long
narrative, in your case. But you need to say, “And I know clients that have
gone through years and years and years of struggle, because they just didn’t
know how to market themselves in such a difficult market. In fact, I know one
guy who lost his business, ended up losing his family, because he couldn’t
market his business.”
Now, I’m getting melodramatic now, but you see how it’s really not that difficult.
Does that help? This whole back-and-forth session is about this. It’s about let
me help you guys think, “Oh, I didn’t think of that.” And that’s cool. If that
helps, awesome.
Is fiction okay legally? Yes. It’s okay legally, as long as it’s based on a
character. In other words, if you just make something completely up,
technically-speaking, the FTC can come down on you, especially if it’s
something like weight loss or financial. They can totally come down on you.
So, I would totally not recommend that.
But if it’s sort of an obscure product or if it’s a service-based thing, then the
odds of getting caught for that are astronomical. Of course, ask your attorney.
I’m not an attorney. But I would certainly suggest that rather than making it
fictional, you do a compilation of people.
So, for example, the story about Jan was actually 3 different people rolled into
one story. Remember, I said the sequencing doesn’t have to be right. The
story of Jan, basically, she was going down pant sizes, and her going into her
closet and feeling miserable. There was actually multiple women that were
feeling that.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 11 --

5
Cheryl asked if there’s another way to build rapport? You bet – the dream
story. Those are the 2 ways to build rapport: the dream story and the
nightmare story.
I found, looking over the best video sales letters that there are, that almost
every one of them, without exception, had some form of a nightmare story.
And it was about the person themselves – and those always converted higher.
Those were always the #1 stories. Always. I can think of every top VSL right
now, every single one of them had some sort of a nightmare story, either about
the person who went through the thing, or it’s a company.
Like I wrote the script for Pimsleur, which is a language learning company. And
you’d think, “Okay, you can learn a language.” Talk about something to build a
nightmare story around.
Well, we didn’t build a nightmare story. Just going through this exercise, which
I did, I asked myself, “Have I ever tried to learn a language?”
Now, I didn’t want to present myself as a first person in this narrative. Even
though I was reading it, I was reading about a company’s product. Which, by
the way, that’s the #1-selling video sales letter out there right now, as far as
language goes.
So, I wanted to make sure that I wasn’t involved in the narrative itself. But I still
went through this exercise.
Have I ever tried to learn a language? Fortunately, I’ve tried to learn many
languages. So, I know how hard this is. I lived in France for a year, and I can
barely, barely speak enough French to get a girl to go on a date with me. It’s
pretty bad. I am not very good with learning languages.
So, I was able to take the pain of my own, that’s not a nightmare story but that’s
a painful story. So, the word “nightmare’s meant to be a little exaggeratory.
In my case that I just read, it was a nightmare. But in the case of learning a
language, it was just an inconvenience. So, all you’d have to do is put
something in to the effect of like, “How many people do you know that struggled
to learn a language? Maybe you have.” You never say, “You have, too.” You
don’t know. “Maybe you have, too.”
“I think most people who have ever tried to learn a language suffer from the
experience. They go through this agony of blah, blah, blah.” And that’s what I
wrote. And that was enough to make me bond to whoever’s reading this.
Because what are they thinking? Someone who wants to learn Spanish, and
they’ve struggled with it, they’re thinking, “Crap, I can’t speak Spanish! I kind of
feel like an idiot.”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 11 --

6
So, when I went through it, I was thinking, “I can’t speak French very well, and I
lived in France. How much of an idiot am I?” So, I don’t want some guy
coming at me going, “Hey, you should learn how to speak French. How easy it
is?” I don’t want them to tell me how easy it is right off the bat. I want them to
tell me that it’s easier than they think, but they can relate to me having a
struggle with it, so that I don’t feel so underneath them. Does that make
sense?
Okay, Lisa asks, “A nightmare story on hospital consulting services?”
Yes, it would definitely have to be from your client’s perspective. And Lisa, I
may be speaking out of your genre of business, but if this has anything to do
with like hospice or how the elderly are taken care of, for example, from my
own mother’s position I could write the worst nightmare story imaginable. I
wouldn’t, out of privacy, but I could say that I could.
So, hospital consulting services, I’m not sure specifically, what you do for
hospitals. If you want to type that into the chat window, so I can answer it more
specifically.
But yeah, I would say that, right off the bat, I’m thinking from the client’s
perspective. What does your client suffer through?
And then, if you want to take a fictional client and say, “Steve is an example.”
Okay, hospital financial and operational consulting services. Thank you for
translating that into English. Helps them reduce their expenses. I’m not a
finance guy, as you might notice.
Lisa, I’ve got one for you.
If you’re helping a hospital reduce their expenses, they’re being attacked by
insurance companies. Hospitals, I would think their biggest enemy would be
insurance companies. The doctors, especially. Hospitals, the equipment’s
going up, the pharmaceutical companies are charging more and more money.
So, I would think that, on some level, you could tap into where is their money
going up, and you could say, “Have you experienced this?” I’m taking it you’re
marketing it to managers. “Have you experienced this as a manager, that
prices continually go up for everything? And it makes your hospital focus less
on taking care of people and more on taking care of the books?”
You could build a story like that. Again, it doesn’t even have to be this long
example. I wanted to give you a long example, so you’d go through the
exercise. And I still want you to go through the exercise, Lisa, because it’s
going to help you write better copy.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 11 --

7
But out of that, you may pull 5 paragraphs, at the most. You could say
something like this, And you could turn this right around to make it first person.
“I was in a hospital recently, and I noticed blank. I noticed that their overhead
was so costly, that there’s fewer doctors on staff, and people were in the ER
room that weren’t getting taken care of.” You see how I can paint that story?
That’s a pretty open story.
So, let’s get one more, because I really want to make sure that you guys,
especially from the guys who say, “I don’t think I have one,” or “I don’t think it’s
going to work for my business.” If we don’t get to you now, we will get to you at
the end of the 30 minutes. And if you just do not think this will work, then what I
want you to do is to do the exercise as if it would work. And then, ask yourself,
“What is the worst pain that my reader or my client is experiencing?” That’s all
you need to think about. What is the worst pain they’re experiencing?
If it’s embarrassment – I’m going through weight loss – they feel imprisoned,
they feel confused, then all you have to do is say, “You know, a lot of people
feel imprisoned, embarrassed and confused by this issue. A lot of people do.
You’re not alone.”
That’s the shortest nightmare story ever, but that’s better than nothing.
I’m telling you, the sales letters that do not have this just do not work. Not
anywhere near as well as the ones that do.
Okay, David, what’s your product? I laugh because I don’t think I’ve ever been
that low. I think I took people to a whole new low with that story. Although, I
would love to hear feedback from you guys. That’s a pretty damned good
story, isn’t it? From an emotional point of view, if weight loss is your interest,
you’ve got to be going, “Man, this guy had it pretty bad.”
David, Internet marketing.
David, did your divorce have anything to do with finances? Yes? There you
go. Bingo.
Almost every divorce does. So, that was not exactly being Kreskin over here, if
you remember Kreskin. That dates me, I guess. That one’s easy.
You could tell your personal story. “I decided to become free from this because
I lost the love of my life, partially because I just didn’t know how to manage my
finances very well.” And you could tell that whole story as emotionally open as
you wish – and, please, be honest with it – or as reserved as you wish. But,
just saying that.
So, this is what I want you guys to walk away from. Just saying what I just said
will pull anyone that’s in that same position into immediate rapport. Because
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 11 --

8
the one thing people who struggle with finances are terrified of is losing their
family.
I’m not trying to be callus here. Please, do not take that as, “Oh, market it at
any expense.” It’s just a fact. People are very concerned with losing their
family, especially guys. Guys are very concerned with their wife is going to be
upset because you can’t pay the bills, and you are having endless fights. Even
if you’re not worried about that, I bet it caused a lot of arguments.
And people who are hitting the site going, “Man, I’m just so freaking sick of
going to work every day and arguing with my wife or my husband,” they’re
going to go, “Oh, I relate to this guy.”
David, I’m not going to share, unless you want me to. I’m not going to share
that with the group. But that would bring me to tears.
This is what I find very interesting, and this is why I love doing 1-on-1
consulting work, even though we’re doing 1-on-200 consulting work now, and
this is something I’ve never done and I will never do again, but I wanted to get
this product out for people because I knew the guys like you, that got on while
they could, would learn a lot from it.
But when you say what you just said, David, and I’m struggling with finances – I
don’t have any children – it still makes me go, “Aw.” It really makes finances be
put into perspective.
So, the way you transition out of that is one word: freedom. Because
increasing your finances is about one thing: freedom. And speaking of
increasing your finances, this is what I’m trying to help everyone on this call do.
I want you all to experience more finances, because it will give you more
freedom. And the best way that I’ve found to do that, is through having a very
good video sales letter. That’s just one of many tools.
But this is a tool that you need in your arsenal. And I guarantee if you do it, you
do it along with us, it’s going to help. And giving people freedom is one of the
greatest joys in my life.
I’m not trying to write another story here, but that is my narrative on that.
By the way, if we did not cover this, and you’re like, “Please, please, please, I’m
desperate,” just type something with asterisks in there and say, “Josh, can you
please cover me,” so Josh can write that down and maybe we can cover that at
the end of the 30 minutes.
A lot of people are like David. “I’m struggling with doing this.” And underneath
it all, they have this amazing, albeit dark story, that needs to be told.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

9
So, please go through these exercises. You’ll get a PDF of this tomorrow.
Probably tonight, actually. But go through the exercisesd. I hope you took
notes. Go through each one of them, and you’ll probably uncover some stuff
that’s really interesting. So, please, do the exercises.
Josh: 	Have you used this to sell a book?
Jon: 	Oh, my gosh, yeah. That’s all I sell. This is my actual nightmare story from
one of my books. Yeah, absolutely.
Josh: 	Can you use stories that you’ve heard or read about in the news?
Jon: 	This is a great question, because it’s completely topical. David Bass and I
have a product called 3X Conversion Formula, which deals with making your
website and your emails get your clicks and more conversions, because more
conversions means more money.
We started off with a news clip that had come out literally the day we were
making our webinar. How fortunate was that? It said exactly what we were
talking about; that Internet neutrality is going in the wrong direction, and that
the ISP’s are taking more and more control over what gets in your in-box.
So, unless you understand this, you will simply not get email deliverability the
way that you used to. You won’t get the clicks.
So, it was perfect. Absolutely, you can use the story from the news. And a
news story, if you’re not comfortable with being this open – and I don’t mean
open like if you’re not there’s something wrong with you, because some people
just don’t like it, that’s cool – then use a news story.
Anything that can make you bond with your user, that’s the key.
Josh: 	How do I deal with creating a story where an assistant normally calls around for
quotes? Paper shredding.
Jon: 	I think I mentioned that in the recording, last session. So, paper shredding.
So, how do I deal with creating a story around a product where an assistant
normally calls around for quotes?
Where will your client see the video sales letter? Will they go to a website?
Obviously, they’re going to go to a website, but what does the assistant calling
for quotes have to do with people seeing the video sales letter? Gina, if you
can shoot those answers over, we will get to it at the end of the 30-minute
session.
But let me go ahead and make something up. Maybe this will help you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 11 --

10
If an assistant is calling around for quotes, and that’s the product that you’re
selling, if that has something to do with that, then I would just tell a story about
an absolutely miserable experience with an assistant calling around for quotes.
If your product makes that easier, take the exact opposite. How many times
have you hired an assistant, only to have her completely come back with the
worst quotes imaginable?
Okay, we are automating the sales process right now. They call a phone
number on the website.
The video sales letter is awesome, then, because a lot of people have a call-in
business. “Here, call toll-free, whatever.” And you collect information, and you
don’t close everyone on the call. Make sure you’re collecting them in a
database, and then a few days later say, “Hey, I think you’ll enjoy this
presentation on (your company), on the service that you sell.”
So, again, they don’t care about the company, they care about the service you
sell. My bad. So, on paper-shredding. Learn about these 5 hidden secrets
about the paper shredding industry. That’s how you can build the nightmare
story, is you take about everything that’s negative, that’s going on in paper
shredding.
For instance, I’m making this up. I don’t know the industry at all. But, for
example, let’s say that you give these sensitive documents to people and they
copy the stuff down. If you’ve heard a story about this, they copy social
security numbers down, somebody copies it, and then they shred it. And then,
all of a sudden, they get their credit card ripped off or their identity ripped off.
That’s it. I’m sold. I’m sold.
You tell that story and you say, “You know what? Unless you have a company
that you can absolutely trust, this happens. Let me tell you a story about a real
client that this really happened to.”
“They thought they were with a reputable firm, and that firm ended up hiring
employees that were nowhere near the standards of our company. Nowhere
near. And their management simply did not have our skill set. And because of
that, those employees ended up ripping off a lot of money from a lot of people.
And what we want to offer you is an exception to that rule. We want to offer
you a totally different experience.”
By the way, that’s what you’re offering even in paper shredding. Notice how I
turned it into something – nothing personal here at all – somewhat bland as
paper shredding into something that’s still an experience, because your client is
still going to experience something. In this case, it’s peace of mind, just like
your USP says.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

11
So, there’s your nightmare story. What’s the opposite of peace of mind?
Torment of mind. So, you have to tell a story that’s around that vein. I know for
a fact, because I know somebody that actually had that happen to them, they
took documents to a shredding company and somebody ripped off their
financial stuff. So, I’m sure that happens all the time.
Ok, great—she said that helped a lot.
